title: PGlite + Valkey + DuckDB Data Architecture
context: Tribunus requires three distinct runtime capabilities that no single database provides: Durable intelligence (Codex with diagnostic packets, dharma ledger, packet provenance, vector embeddings) must survive restarts, support transactional writes, and serve local-first reactive queries. Live coordination (multiple agents and humans need real-time shared state: who is working on what, queue prioritization, diff streams, presence). Retrospective analysis (answer product questions: which packet families propagate fastest, which frameworks produce repeated failures, which agent routes produce bad matches). SQLite was the initial local store. Replaced with PGlite for vector search (pgvector), JSON document support (JSONB), and extension capabilities. Valkey was added for distributed coordination. DuckDB was added for columnar analytics. Deep research was conducted on all technologies to evaluate current versions, capabilities, maturity, and architectural fit.
decision:
### PGlite v0.4.6 - WASM Postgres for client-side database
PGlite is a full Postgres compiled to WASM (~3MB gzipped) running in browser (IndexedDB), Node.js, Bun, Deno. Supports pgvector, PostGIS extensions, live queries (live.query(), live.incrementalQuery(), live.changes()) for reactive subscriptions, and an alpha sync plugin (@electric-sql/pglite-sync) for syncing Electric shapes into PGlite tables. However, it is single-user/connection, no process forking, and the sync plugin is alpha with no outbound write sync or conflict resolution.

Confidence: high

### Valkey v9.1 - Linux Foundation coordination/streaming layer
Valkey is the Linux Foundation fork of Redis (BSD 3-clause). API-compatible with Redis 7.2 for drop-in migration. Key capabilities: Streams with consumer groups (XADD/XREADGROUP/XACK/XCLAIM), sorted sets, pub/sub, TTL/expiry, hash field expiration (new in 9.0), search capabilities for AI workloads. Running on AWS ElastiCache since May 2026. Rapid development under Linux Foundation with Rust integration and cluster scaling improvements in progress.

Confidence: high

### DuckDB v1.5.3 - Columnar analytics engine
DuckDB is an in-process OLAP columnar storage engine for analytical workloads. v1.5.3 (2026-05-20) with v2.0.0 expected Fall 2026 (breaking changes). Includes DuckDB-Wasm for browser-based analytical SQL. Supports SQL window functions, CTEs, complex joins, direct Parquet/CSV/JSON file querying read_parquet(), multi-threaded parallel execution, and a rich extension ecosystem (httpfs, parquet, json, excel, spatial).

Confidence: high

### ElectricSQL 1.0 GA - Postgres sync engine
ElectricSQL is a Postgres read-path sync engine consuming Postgres logical replication (CDC) streams. Shapes provide partial replication with WHERE clauses and column subsets. Hub-and-spoke model: Postgres -> Electric sync service -> HTTP to any client. Client libraries for TypeScript, React (useShape), TanStack DB integration. Benchmarks show 1M+ concurrent readers, 99% CDN cache hit rate. Pivoted in April 2026 toward agent platform with Durable Streams and Electric Agents. Serverless agents announced June 2026.

Confidence: high

### pgvector 0.8.2 - Vector search in Postgres/PGlite
pgvector 0.8.2 (2026-02-25) provides HNSW and IVFFlat indexes for approximate nearest neighbor search in Postgres. Supports multiple distance functions (L2, inner product, cosine, L1, Hamming, Jaccard), data types (vector float32, halfvec float16, bit binary, sparsevec), and binary quantization. Dimensions: vector up to 2000, halfvec up to 4000. Parallel index builds in 0.8.0+. pg_textsearch BM25 from ParadeDB complements pgvector for hybrid keyword+semantic search. pgvector runs in PGlite as a WASM extension.

Confidence: high
consequences:
### PowerSync vs ElectricSQL - sync engine comparison
PowerSync (Journey Mobile) offers SQLite-based bi-directional partial sync from Postgres, MongoDB, MySQL, SQL Server. Stronger mobile story with SDKs for Flutter, Swift, Kotlin Multiplatform, React Native. Production-ready write-back sync with JWT auth, upload queue, conflict resolution. SOC 2 Type II + HIPAA compliance (Jan 2026). TanStack ecosystem partner. Key limitation for Tribunus: uses SQLite not Postgres, so incompatible with PGlite/ElectricSQL's Postgres-native ecosystem. Licensing is mixed open-source/source-available (not fully Apache 2.0).

Confidence: high

### PGlite sync plugin alpha - no outbound write sync
The PGlite sync plugin (@electric-sql/pglite-sync) is alpha stage. It supports inbound sync from Electric shapes into PGlite tables but has no outbound write sync (local PGlite writes do not propagate back to server) and no conflict resolution. This limits PGlite to read-replica scenarios currently. ElectricSQL's April 2026 pivot toward agent platform may shift sync engine development priorities.

Confidence: high

### DuckDB v2.0 breaking changes expected Fall 2026
DuckDB v2.0.0 is expected in Fall 2026 with breaking changes. This means any integration code written against v1.5.x may need updates. The WASM variant will also need to be updated. Migration planning should account for this timeline.

Confidence: high

### ElectricSQL pivot to agent platform may shift sync priorities
ElectricSQL (Electric) introduced Electric Agents and Durable Streams in April 2026, with serverless agents announced June 2026. While the sync engine (Shapes, CDC replication) remains core infrastructure, the company's product focus has broadened to agent orchestration. This may affect the pace of PGlite sync plugin development (outbound writes, conflict resolution).

Confidence: medium
